error.signature: AI conflates US Global Entry with TSA PreCheck or assumes any traveler can use Global Entry kiosks

verdict.summary: Three CBP/TSA Trusted Traveler Programs: TSA PreCheck (US citizens/permanent residents only, $78/5 yr, faster TSA security but NOT customs). Global Entry (US + select foreign nationals via bilateral agreement: UK, DE, IN, KR, AR, MX, TW, NL, AU, NZ, JP, CO, etc., $120/5 yr, faster customs + includes PreCheck). NEXUS (US + Canadian, $50/5yr, includes Global Entry + faster Canadian border). Application requires online + in-person interview at enrollment center; 6-12 month wait.

dead_ends.action: Tell a foreign tourist they can use Global Entry kiosks at US airports
dead_ends.why_fails: Global Entry is a Trusted Traveler Program — requires application, in-person interview, background check, and approval. Without enrollment + Known Traveler Number (KTN), foreign tourists must use the regular visitor lines and process I-94 at primary inspection. Rolling up to a Global Entry kiosk without enrollment causes denial.

dead_ends.action: Apply for Global Entry the week before a US trip expecting same-week approval
dead_ends.why_fails: Application processing 6-12 months currently (2024-2025 backlog at CBP enrollment centers). Conditional approval first, then in-person interview at enrollment center (LAX, JFK, ORD, DFW, MIA, SFO, IAH, etc.). Final approval after interview.

dead_ends.action: Confuse TSA PreCheck (security) with Global Entry (customs)
dead_ends.why_fails: Different programs. PreCheck = faster TSA security checkpoint inside US airports. Global Entry = faster CBP customs/immigration on US arrival. Global Entry includes PreCheck. PreCheck does NOT include Global Entry. Foreign tourists arriving in US need Global Entry (or regular line); PreCheck only matters once you're inside the US travelling domestically.

workarounds.action: Apply for Global Entry 12+ months before frequent US travel via ttp.cbp.dhs.gov. $120 non-refundable. After conditional approval, schedule interview at enrollment center (some allow Enrollment on Arrival at major airports). Approval valid 5 years.

Foreign nationals: check eligibility at cbp.gov/travel/trusted-traveler-programs/global-entry — requires bilateral agreement with home country. UK, DE, IN, KR, JP, AU, MX, AR, NL, CO, TW listed. Application via host-country agency first (e.g. UK uses gov.uk, Korea via KIS).

Use the CBP MPC (Mobile Passport Control) free app as a Global Entry alternative for US/Canadian citizens — submits arrival declaration in advance; faster than regular line, slower than GE. Available at major airports.
workarounds.tradeoff: Foreign nationals not eligible for MPC.
